clean_body: Instead of noise, Trump is making moves in silence. His next target — Pennsylvania. | | | | | == | Peter, Donald Trump doesn't need to win the headlines to win back the White House. He tried chaos on Jan. 6 and it failed. He tried a loud, billion-dollar Supreme Court race in Wisconsin earlier this year -- and Democrats shut him down. So now he's learned his lesson - and he and his MAGA Extremists are still plotting their takeover. Instead of noise, Trump is making moves in silence. His next target -- Pennsylvania. | | == Three Democratic justices are on the ballot this November in a retention election most voters don't even know is happening. If MAGA groups can flip those seats, they'll seize control of the Pennsylvania Supreme Court. And once they do, they'll have the power to redraw maps, restrict voting rights, and toss out legal challenges -- paving the way for Trump in 2028. This is Trump's hidden path to a third term. No rallies. No riots. Just three quiet "No" votes on a November ballot. Defend Our Courts is one of the only groups exposing this plan. But we're being outspent and are outnumbered -- and if we wait until October to act, it will be too late. == | | | | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | | | | == We know how to beat him. clean_body: Your battle plan against the radical Left (learn more inside)| | | Friend, If you've ever wondered whether we can actually win this fight for America's soul... Let me send you the proof. I want to ship you a FREE hardcover copy of my book, Right Wing Revolution. No strings attached, just make a donation of ANY amount to Turning Point USA today so that we can reach even more young people and fuel our unstoppable movement. This isn't just a book. It 's what the Left never wanted you to read. Why? Because it lays out -- in brutal detail -- how they hijacked our culture... And how we can flip it BACK, one campus, one student, one bold conversation at a time. There 's no better time than NOW for you to join us... Because we're seeing it in "Red" states and "Blue" states: 🔥 Students standing tall for this country 🔥 Overflowing crowds shouting that they're "PROUD to be an American!"🔥 Hands in the air to get their own "47" and "MAGA" hats We've sparked a wildfire. And it's only growing...Right Wing Revolution tells the story behind the movement. How we keep the momentum going. How we DEFEAT the Left once and for all. Friend, this isn't about merely watching history... It's about making it. Let's win this fight. Let's make history. Let's never let the Left have its grip on our nation's youth again! The next generation is choosing America -- and with your help, we will make sure they never look back. For America, Charlie Kirk Founder & CEO Turning Point USA 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

We’re looking at full completion towards the end of the decade, early next decade, to finish all 14 components of the LEAP program.”

Carreno said the project does not have a price tag yet, though he admitted it will be “significantly higher” than the $800 million that the airlines operating at the airport already agreed to pay for their portion of the costs. The previous major improvement project for Love Field, the Love Field Modernization Program, was valued at $519 million, officials said.

The carrier operates 18 of the airport’s 20 gates, with Atlanta-based Delta Airlines operating the other two.

Love Field [had nearly 18 million passengers last year](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eIMSJymr_XNwmjpkZ0j78ZXB3UQ0li9LELElf28q5nA5E-0wOVRnC3BcNgxMcfLpuuLNRszb-H54NubpxIGLHiibxTg9g6wqoG20rbD1KbjiJrCV1WskZvTLkzRe_tgyxZYtUjCWoqo_KGxT5-nNuN-glY7oJmhIhotvRpyP_T7wSexUFYmqy6iqk8r06ODl2hNBCo1BUCdW3KhfmoSVpxXI_uDdpHOyiwtvwD_crBFmFcsMqZDjCTbCJVHzldmV8s0D1CtMx4rHKc1SaRNK3pevHFQqtqsJXSL2UxloXyODasS6zafrma3MS_E7dL7o6R_uMTXxbk-rjwCrMsYF6komCfW0ZWyc4qkRhFyQkIEU8bePBcxXqjKhcj41-QWfNtbFjJmdYQJ9OXgbYP0YAAuUe_MP0a8t1LPY5qc8WKGS7viEbvFAt6bXX-_tOku6bXoYO06l5ODnInYFxA0wflwIYpXZFJRPzeByGk-Pfe7VNUU6NpBhvyv5NtgKirv3Wg/4jd/e6EPCqhCTEqY1FEVzq4SFA/h22/h001.Z_FMlq6fvxgNIMXJhftUFDJE1pnji-9X6BWXIEUlm98). The current terminal complex, designed in 2013, has a designed capacity for 8 million passenger boardings. By 2018, capacity had already exceeded that level, officials said. The increased capacity came on the heels of the 2014 repeal of the Wright Amendment, initially passed in 1979 to restrict the airport so that it could not compete with the larger DFW International Airport. After the law was repealed, Southwest could offer nonstop flights to any destination in the U.S.

Despite the repeal, some [restrictions of the law](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_lEtQPc4m2J7qER2AIMiECr9zwVRdwvjDKhffGw5nU08b0n0iro0NtE_p1YOYldlSioKftwAeNB0QFV1KSGo2eyH68pzHEFnK0qv6sxw_FlWFadJC-jNvq3UisncFhOcWZXHZKnzvqCfxf0L8cJUx9SLfwBTj08zLR0TtwID8ki7YMfurLpugKwRLle9QBJJsSBTnbAFuodEMgtG4c-GzegoF5Qn4ySwxq6rzy3YI59rjgZliVJEk44mR7bH4McTkOnp-QRy0GM1zmlp1Yn_93tqLY5exAp5Yk_wO61MRdnBmbmm5Wd01g_ZHRdu4lgUt5_zYZYicGbrHxFauVUFqQeJASk_TRYWy2cKzH9N-9ot7YJ9qUmV3qJc8pK3t5BbngL678TocyA_cBe4VRkLd_kbXKlKHuWeMYyClZWyXJFc5LrI7ou3xuXhO8WQat6il_NKe2_VdzfYW91Xl73IQJdNvX6ah6X0XiXt05e69mw6DGP4pQrW0dOLMvGFyVft0pt2ayh7SXOmQ_6h_o2J-OELhrU2VnsMsWZUlaDUYjHiA/4jd/e6EPCqhCTEqY1FEVzq4SFA/h23/h001.CrN3FCO11Ii13SZIdGd5tBxbKRPdXacFc6Pi6X1ofgo) are still enforced through an agreement between the city of Dallas, the city of Fort Worth, Southwest Airlines, American Airlines and the DFW Airport Board.

The agreement stipulates that Love Field is limited to 20 gates, cannot offer international passenger service and had to negotiate a voluntary noise curfew with commercial airlines. According to Carreno, the airport is the only one in the country with a federal law restricting the number of gates it can have.

Despite the restrictions, Love Field is now the [32nd busiest airport in the U.S](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0ePcHeOe8SXjMpthyhK47ZsOXrxyvYceG2qMIRG-XmUrXxiirKdw9Z2_Pmt1m0XSrzhfQaEHh98fSZUbfljhubb2bzgFfOqoNHWmEUjzK1kKoIQD-ExmryZFCjiMuA87De8ySLMJYrJ5DssqTeoNLDdlT10pOW15eO6U5y3epG3rMeNmRM9F318VtXaErhhgk4y2tW-6sahx3EX3P9PMD4bgp13rdk9fPMejs3LmaFskiZ5OmwyAisRqyCcfxotiCNaoKTQElIyXu7ZzNKKfxMHj30lpKyXcwzQ2U5YCCTlyrlh5k1c0_MAq5xtInTITWbntqm4CCrULVD1LgIYm4BCEpLrwQiaahzMvEkuLeyzEDq-Ioo2nhWkwc2TkBHQ8Og39dRCdP7zO7M4F519Tgp5WB2asAUFEN8h7Ddbiigc-pZPlPF0yoLhrDFq6aY_UqPNgsW9XiLu-6EawNlhmJD-hacTpZzHu_OsD_55KtGRk7-pHobFPLV7lspIHrtv-PGQSxPOWVSVKWxSRd5tNZuHg/4jd/e6EPCqhCTEqY1FEVzq4SFA/h24/h001.Bddcb6tGJ80NYBbuz4D61W16e744pfMR-tyc6r33xXA). The airport forecasts it could eventually handle 11.9 million passenger boardings and nearly 24 million passengers total under the gate cap, Carreno said.

“We think that’s what is possible,” Carreno said. “So this program is looking at how we get ourselves to that activity level.”

Carreno said Love Field could get close to reaching those passenger estimates by early next decade. “That’s why we want to fast-track this program and make sure that we can accommodate and continue to add passengers into this airport.”" [DMN](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_lEtQPc4m2J7qER2AIMiECrPMcEEA9AZSexgUGruGoQZLHYGAqyzH1Ooio0WnEk5DR9yB6vA-sYXbdZCE6gS4YkZS896TAjV09f4QMgc5fJrF2F0lHxJru3Tw2GILmEgAELXyFkzDZikMQUcouDd_Z7me-zLfyY1V30b2oaRdhmOITQH7z0NdhmvSmvb-aKIABt_1mCdAl03lreI01G4vp9lyVJIiW4DEiHqZqv0I-wiZk-ULf6BnvzlHoENCuhkvibCvdSJS4e688z3szCz5Y0sc5hAZZaHNyK5TAg-lwu6gQ9obBLcmxl0MFJYiqoIKduUcitsyFaPB413CtVtUQyTR7uiSiRZ8WVLiSobbkCGjdstcg7eOjILCsHVn0TtfLY2jMVE8LDall-QayhTBsQDmjJEr4jPPX85k0B9GR2We9bilLhOh4a9ivjJ3e0BTto_58sguOd8tTBxoVEmCE63UIo3d-KYFy71FvVlZsOyAvPpgP5cC6kJy5B3A0mZ2TntSOB80vpeI_44occNzJ8CtatIIml-IsASGjoppbm7fldvQ-Fa0Nh21Pfe-ezwfXGgHhZMNYjIXvMqM4jDvPn/4jd/e6EPCqhCTEqY1FEVzq4SFA/h25/h001.3SwF6UNLm0rrzTdQx63H28hfklOB40yEKWJ7P5V82AU) ($)

Experience news without the noise; let 1440 help you make up your own mind. Sign up now and invite your friends and family to be part of the informed. | TOP NEWS “Love Field unveils overhaul plan that could add 50% more passengers,” Dallas Morning News' Jordan Parker \-- " leaders are calling for a major overhaul to expand the airport in an attempt to squeeze more passengers into the size-restricted air hub. Plans call for expanding the terminal, building a new facility for TSA security checkpoints and new baggage claim and adding a fourth parking garage, among other changes. » That could help the airport increase passenger traffic by almost 50% while keeping the legally mandated 20 gates. The expansion program, dubbed , is entering its final stages of development and to the Dallas City Council last week. The airport, which is owned by the city of Dallas and hosts the headquarters for , is now determining the financial feasibility of the project and final layout details. Patrick Carreno , the airport’s aviation director, told The Dallas Morning News that the Dallas Love Field Expansion Airport Program’s includes a 50-foot extension of the existing terminal, a consolidated rental car facility, a new terminal headhouse for ticketing, TSA security checkpoints and baggage claim, a new parking garage and a commercial pickup area for transportation companies such as Uber and Lyft. » A skybridge will connect passengers between security and the main terminal, Carreno said. New access roadways and elevated roadways to improve the flow of traffic around the airport are also part of the plans. Officials that the plan would “enhance capacity, elevate the passenger experience and prepare the City of Dallas-owned airport for continued long-term growth.” A team will begin designing the project early next year, with “full construction” set to begin in the first quarter of 2027, Carreno said. “We don’t have the full timeline yet. We’re looking at full completion towards the end of the decade, early next decade, to finish all 14 components of the LEAP program.” Carreno said the project does not have a price tag yet, though he admitted it will be “significantly higher” than the $800 million that the airlines operating at the airport already agreed to pay for their portion of the costs. The previous major improvement project for Love Field, the Love Field Modernization Program, was valued at $519 million, officials said. | A screenshot of a video of preliminary plans to redesign Dallas Love Field to accommodate more passengers, which was presented to Dallas officials last week. (Courtesy Dallas Love Field) The announcement of the plan comes three months after . The carrier operates 18 of the airport’s 20 gates, with Atlanta-based Delta Airlines operating the other two. Love Field . The current terminal complex, designed in 2013, has a designed capacity for 8 million passenger boardings. By 2018, capacity had already exceeded that level, officials said. The increased capacity came on the heels of the 2014 repeal of the Wright Amendment, initially passed in 1979 to restrict the airport so that it could not compete with the larger DFW International Airport. After the law was repealed, Southwest could offer nonstop flights to any destination in the U.S. Despite the repeal, some are still enforced through an agreement between the city of Dallas, the city of Fort Worth, Southwest Airlines, American Airlines and the DFW Airport Board. The agreement stipulates that Love Field is limited to 20 gates, cannot offer international passenger service and had to negotiate a voluntary noise curfew with commercial airlines. According to Carreno, the airport is the only one in the country with a federal law restricting the number of gates it can have. Despite the restrictions, Love Field is now the . The airport forecasts it could eventually handle 11.9 million passenger boardings and nearly 24 million passengers total under the gate cap, Carreno said. “We think that’s what is possible,” Carreno said. “So this program is looking at how we get ourselves to that activity level.” Carreno said Love Field could get close to reaching those passenger estimates by early next decade. “That’s why we want to fast-track this program and make sure that we can accommodate and continue to add passengers into this airport.”" ($) | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. name: Jon Ossoff
subject: New York Times: "People are going silent..."
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Peter,
Before I ran for the U.S. Senate, I was an investigative journalist leading a team that exposed corruption and human rights abuses worldwide.
This report in _The New York Times_ reminded me of what I saw in many of those investigations:
[](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-august25.AuYWG3&refcode=em250826-1-f&t=1&akid=681%2E5691347%2EdTUR3y)
More and more, the atmosphere under Trump reminds me of countries where dissent is silenced by intimidation.
From the article:
_Fired federal workers who are worried about losing their homes ask not to be quoted by name. University presidents fearing that millions of dollars in federal funding could disappear are holding their fire. Chief executives alarmed by tariffs that could hurt their businesses are on mute. ..._
_“When you see important societal actors — be it university presidents, media outlets, C.E.O.s, mayors, governors — changing their behavior in order to avoid the wrath of the government, that’s a sign that we’ve crossed the line into some form of authoritarianism,” said Steven Levitsky, a professor of government at Harvard and the co-author of the influential 2018 book “How Democracies Die.”_
**This is unacceptable in the United States of America.**
Peter — As Trump dismantles checks on his power and purges the government to fill every office with loyalists, more and more Americans are too afraid of retaliation to speak up.
**This descent into an un-American America must be stopped. We must regain Congressional power.**
I’m the ONLY Democratic Senator up for re-election in a state Trump won.
That means I’m MAGA’s #1 target for defeat.
**Help me beat them again, just like we did in 2021.**
[**Please contribute to my campaign today.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-august25.AuYWG3&refcode=em250826-1-f&t=2&akid=681%2E5691347%2EdTUR3y)
Thank you for chipping in if you can.
\- Jon
**[DONATE](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-august25.AuYWG3&refcode=em250826-1-f&t=3&akid=681%2E5691347%2EdTUR3y)**

Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thank you for supporting Jon Ossoff. Email is a critical way to keep up with Jon and his work in the US Senate, but you can click [here](https://act.electjon.com/signup/fewer-emails-signup/?t=4&akid=681%2E5691347%2EdTUR3y) to sign up to receive fewer emails, or click [here](https://act.electjon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=681%2E5691347%2EdTUR3y) if you'd like to unsubscribe.

# [The Grit of Entrepreneurship](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=171985842&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTg1ODQyLCJpYXQiOjE3NTYyMjEzOTUsImV4cCI6MTc1ODgxMzM5NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.x2qQPG9A-VHTcpMhJxb36VKdWkT2ztQKuZZ0QWmMBNg)
### Most start-ups will fail. Why you should do it anyway.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Aug 26
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=171985842&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTg1ODQyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NjIyMTM5NSwiZXhwIjoxNzU4ODEzMzk1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.hKGDWkhRXWiiMNlliI5t46aT7edRb3CgNZNdoSv4_N0&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=171985842&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTg1ODQyLCJpYXQiOjE3NTYyMjEzOTUsImV4cCI6MTc1ODgxMzM5NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.x2qQPG9A-VHTcpMhJxb36VKdWkT2ztQKuZZ0QWmMBNg&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack..-B-oB_uqs_iECFv-Eqe1pg1KZxOw31r4h5EsPx_cmNs?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/the-grit-of-entrepreneurship?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

It takes more than grit to start a small business.
I've been lecturing at UPenn's Wharton School of Business since 2022.
Many of my classes are held in Huntsman Hall, named after Jon Huntsman Sr, a poor kid from Idaho who earned a scholarship to Penn, graduating in 1959, who went on to serve in the Navy, and eventually became a self-made billionaire. The significance of that building named after a Navy Vet is not lost on me.
Roughly 10% of Wharton's first-year MBA students are veterans. I remind them that half of WWII veterans eventually started small businesses. Out of that wave came brands like Walmart, Comcast, and Nike.
But today, [fewer than 5%](https://substack.com/redirect/a17ca80d-4c4f-4607-a47a-9a6e9f1cf403?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of post-9/11 veterans start their own business.
From the Desk of Honorable Patrick J. Murphy is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..J3iYOyyvMIhX95xdVqhb3cUYvsM9qefwizFNnnR7u7U?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=171985842)
## **Most Will Fail**
Every year, [595,000 small businesses ](https://substack.com/redirect/ffd28643-7ba4-4bcb-8cee-01902772bb00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)shut down.
According to the [Chamber of Commerce](https://substack.com/redirect/ffd28643-7ba4-4bcb-8cee-01902772bb00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* 18% fail within the first year
* 50% fail by year five
* 65% fail by year ten

So why encourage anyone to try if the odds are stacked against them?
Because _fortune favors the bold._ Or, as I tell my Wharton students:_Fortis Fortuna Adiuvat. (_ It just sounds better in Latin.)
Here's the truth: success isn't just luck, though luck certainly helps. It's about moral courage--the decision to act when it's easier to sit it out.
I've been lucky, no question. I was born into a loving family with two phenomenal, Church-going, working-class parents in the greatest country in the world. They ensured I had teachers, priests, and lawyers who mentored me when I needed it most. My Mom insisted my brother and I took the bus and 'El' (elevated train) into Center City Philly every day (over an hour each way) to be messengers at a law firm at ages 14-18, even though we already had a paper route and were playing high school hockey. She wanted us to be around leaders we wouldn't usually come in contact with.
But let's be clear, I've also had more than my fair share of failures. I did summer school in high school. _Twice._ Was rejected from King's College, which meant starting out at community college. Law School did not come easily to me. I was not selected to join the 82nd Division until ten years after I first put it on my 'dream sheet.' Was dumped by a girlfriend during my summer basic training, and then dumped by another one when I left for advanced training, oh, & then my fiance the day I came back from war. I've been rejected by top law firms. Lost two elections. Divorced. The list goes on.
And yet, when I look back, the moments that defined my success are greater than the sum of my biggest fails. And it wasn't about luck. It was about boldness and grit.
**I wouldn 't be where I am today if I didn't have the grit to: **
* Volunteer for combat--twice.
* Jump out of airplanes with an M4 strapped to my side.
* Run for Congress with $322 in my checking account.
* Start half dozen small businesses that stumbled, before achieving financial success.

None of it was easy. All of it required risk. And eventually, some of it paid off.
Looking back, I can say without a doubt in my mind: fortune _does_ favor the bold.
Thomas Jefferson put it best: _" I am a great believer in luck, and I find the harder I work the more I have of it."_
He was right.
And that's why I tell my students: yes, if you start a business, most will fail. But some will build something extraordinary. And the only way to find out which camp you're in is to take the leap.
## **Entrepreneurship Matters**
This past weekend, I had the honor of giving the keynote at the American Legion National Convention in Tampa, Florida. 4,000 Legionnaires from across the country gathered to live out our motto: _" For God and Country." _
Here's the big picture I painted to their Task Force on Small Business:
* There are **32.5 million small businesses in America**.
* They make up **99.9% of all businesses.**
* They employ **46% of the American workforce.**

Small businesses are the backbone of America.
## **How Do You Even Start?**
There's no single path to entrepreneurship. Some people start with an original idea. Others buy an existing company--a trend that will only accelerate with the coming "silver tsunami" of retiring Baby Boomer business owners. An estimated [4.5 million privately held businesses are expected to transition within the next decade](https://substack.com/redirect/8c3bf650-4924-4a94-aeb6-2b8d3efd59fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), representing nearly $14 trillion in wealth at stake. And some rise through the ranks of a company before eventually striking out on their own. Why do so many fail?
* Lack of market research
* Poor business plan
* Insufficient capital
* Weak marketing
* Inadequate management

The list is long, but the takeaway is simple: success requires more than grit. It requires preparation, discipline, and support.
When I think of entrepreneurs I admire, two stand out.
* **Phil Knight, founder of Nike.** He took a scrappy idea--importing Japanese running shoes--and built it into one of the most iconic brands in the world. His story is a reminder that relentless persistence can turn a humble start into a global empire. He's an Army veteran who co-founded Nike with his legendary track coach Bill Bowerman, a fellow Army veteran who earned the Silver Star in Europe during WWII and came home to become a track coach.

* **Blake Hall, founder of ID.me.** A Vanderbilt Army ROTC grad and Ranger Regiment scout platoon leader in Iraq, he earned the Bronze Star for Valor. When he told his battalion commander he planned to leave the Army for Harvard Business School, he found his next mission and was encouraged to go crush it. Some of his first hires were the teammates he served with. Today, ID.me is valued at over $2 billion and serves tens of millions of Americans verifying their identity online.

The point is role models matter. If they can do it, so can you. But you gotta plan your work and tirelessly, relentlessly work your plan.
## **Building the Infrastructure**
The good news is, no one has to do this alone. There's an entire ecosystem designed to help veterans and entrepreneurs take the leap.
* **Bunker Labs** operates in 31 cities, helping veterans develop business plans and secure capital. They are part of the Syracuse University D'Aniello Institute for Veterans and Military Families, led by Air Force Vet Barb Carson, their managing director, who joined us in Tampa.
* The **U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA)** supports more than 900 regional development centers, often located at community colleges and state universities, offering resources and training. The SBA Deputy Administrator Bill Burns joined us in Tampa.
* The SBA also runs **SCORE** , the nation's largest volunteer business-mentorship network, founded in 1964. It provides free or low-cost workshops led by experienced entrepreneurs.
* The VA also has a Veterans First initiative to help budding Vetrepreneurs and the 1.5 million veteran owned companies across America. They also showed up in Tampa, along with many private sector leaders providing much-needed guidance to veterans wouldnt feel overwhelmed or drown in the sea of goodwill.

In other words, the scaffolding is there. The challenge is how to best use it.
## **Why I Focus on Veterans**
I'll be the first to admit: I wasn't the best soldier or paratrooper. Many of my colleagues ran faster, though I'm still proud my personal two-mile run was consistently under the 11:56 standard. As someone who first joined the Army at 135 pounds, many of my colleagues could do more push-ups, but I worked my way up and had no problem doing the Division-standard 82 push-up minimum. At first I was intimidated by my colleagues who came from elite schools like Harvard or West Point (not that Bucks County Community College isn't awesome). Over time, I realized I was every bit their equal. I was just as good, if not better, as a soldier. I loved the grind and being around my warrior teammates.
Here's what else I know: serving our country in uniform isn't something people generally do, and most people sure as hell don't want their kids to do it. Most young Americans do not have the propensity to serve, either (I'll save the why for a later column).
That's why I've dedicated so much of my life to opening doors for veterans and solving the obstacles they face. I don't just cultivate relationships with veterans, I teach, mentor, and invest in them. Through Wharton. Through our venture capital funds that have invested over $250 million in 200 companies--90% veteran-led. Through my show _Warrior Money,_ the number one business podcast on Yahoo Finance. Actions speak louder than words.
Because I know if they can see it, they can be it.
When I work with veterans, I try to cultivate one thing above all else: a growth mindset. They already bring discipline and a purpose-driven life shaped by service. They know how to live by their principles under pressure. Those values are desperately needed in corporate America today. Statistically, veterans who start businesses are more likely to succeed than their peers.
So who knows? The next Ralph Lauren, the next Wendy's, the next Enterprise Rent-A-Car--it might come from one of my post-9/11 brothers or sisters in arms. Maybe even someone I've served with or taught in class.
And I'll keep doing everything I can to make sure they get the chance.
It's not just about business, either. I also push veterans to step into public service. As I wrote in a past [Substack](https://substack.com/redirect/ab682478-57f1-458f-90e9-314259d9cd68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Congress has gone from over 80% veterans after WWII to just 18% today. That's a crisis for our democracy. We need those voices back in the room. If you want to help, you can give to the [Taking the Hill PAC](https://substack.com/redirect/8eb8238f-4a43-4384-b840-46f5007b3bb3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which I founded in 2018.
## **Anniversary of my Namesake**
This week marks the anniversary of my namesake's passing, Army Specialist Patrick Edward Ward, who we lost on August 22, 1968. He earned the Silver Star in Vietnam, sacrificing his life to save his brothers in combat.
He was my mom's best friend--a Roman Catholic football player who wrote joyful letters and recorded cassette tapes to her while she was in the convent becoming a Catholic nun (before she met my dad). He described how he would appropriate food and supplies to give to the poor Vietnamese children at their local orphanage.
I like to think he's smiling down from Heaven. In Baghdad, my men and I handed out colorful toy glasses to Iraqi kids. That small gesture was done in his spirit.
## **Carry A Torch For Those Who Need It**
Now for a more difficult topic.
I often think about the 19 men I served with in Iraq who never made it home. I think about the eight passengers who died on the 2015 Amtrak 188 derailment, while I was just knocked unconscious. I think about the guys I grew up with in Philly who didn't make it to their twenties.
That kind of loss can sit heavy if you let it. For me, I've tried to harness that guilt, that remorse, and that strange sense of luck to be more disciplined.
As my Wharton colleague Adam Grant writes in his book _Hidden Potential_ , people with more discipline often use the least amount of it. It's less about willpower and more about the "scaffolding" in your life to overcome obstacles. It's easier to carry a torch for those who matter to us. That's where strength comes from.
So, today I carry a torch for Patrick Ward. For the 19 paratroopers I lost. For the eight passengers on Amtrak 188 who died, and the hundreds of thousands living with PTSD. My torch is for the veterans I teach and invest in. That torch can be heavy, but it often gives me strength. If we carry the torch for one another, we will discover strength we didn't know we had.

_Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here:_ [ATXpulsepremium.com](https://substack.com/redirect/4f42c6f0-2614-4898-96c1-34d6cbbc4b01?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Read the most recent FREE subscriber email [here](https://substack.com/redirect/3b878725-6b85-47b7-8056-395bb6660f76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr [here](https://substack.com/redirect/4f42c6f0-2614-4898-96c1-34d6cbbc4b01?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

**" Love Field unveils overhaul plan that could add 50% more passengers," **Dallas Morning News' **Jordan Parker** \-- "[Dallas Love Field](https://substack.com/redirect/ae501a29-5a78-49da-99a3-ec24a10549d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) leaders are calling for a major overhaul to expand the airport in an attempt to squeeze more passengers into the size-restricted air hub.

Plans call for expanding the terminal, building a new facility for TSA security checkpoints and new baggage claim and adding a fourth parking garage, among other changes.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/a0b6776c-93d8-45a8-8026-276e84f25358?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
That could help the airport increase passenger traffic by almost 50% while keeping the legally mandated 20 gates.

The expansion program, dubbed [Love What's Next](https://substack.com/redirect/94c507af-0f45-43f8-b549-53c74aa5cec7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), is entering its final stages of development and [was presented](https://substack.com/redirect/5c781576-dcbb-4f2d-b66a-f74cab3f9470?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the Dallas City Council last week. The airport, which is owned by the city of Dallas and hosts the headquarters for [Southwest Airlines](https://substack.com/redirect/41b5fbd9-538f-489d-881f-53cd383cc8e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), is now determining the financial feasibility of the project and final layout details.

**Patrick Carreno** , the airport's aviation director, told _The Dallas Morning News_ that the Dallas Love Field Expansion Airport Program's [initial design](https://substack.com/redirect/31fb4e1d-5a63-414c-83e0-56b919dfa7d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) includes a 50-foot extension of the existing terminal, a consolidated rental car facility, a new terminal headhouse for ticketing, TSA security checkpoints and baggage claim, a new parking garage and a commercial pickup area for transportation companies such as Uber and Lyft.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/a0b6776c-93d8-45a8-8026-276e84f25358?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
A skybridge will connect passengers between security and the main terminal, Carreno said. New access roadways and elevated roadways to improve the flow of traffic around the airport are also part of the plans.

Officials [expressed hope last week](https://substack.com/redirect/0aa42d2c-8195-49e3-ac4b-015e5a413173?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that the plan would "enhance capacity, elevate the passenger experience and prepare the City of Dallas-owned airport for continued long-term growth." A team will begin designing the project early next year, with "full construction" set to begin in the first quarter of 2027, Carreno said. "We don't have the full timeline yet. We're looking at full completion towards the end of the decade, early next decade, to finish all 14 components of the LEAP program."

Carreno said the project does not have a price tag yet, though he admitted it will be "significantly higher" than the $800 million that the airlines operating at the airport already agreed to pay for their portion of the costs. The previous major improvement project for Love Field, the Love Field Modernization Program, was valued at $519 million, officials said.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/df1583d6-5242-42a3-8e51-6f314bba3138?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A screenshot of a video of preliminary plans to redesign Dallas Love Field to accommodate more passengers, which was presented to Dallas officials last week. (Courtesy Dallas Love Field)
The announcement of the plan comes three months after [Southwest Airlines solidified its commitment to Love Field as its home base through at least 2040](https://substack.com/redirect/01ec7fa0-960f-40ad-aeec-0f23a9c38346?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The carrier operates 18 of the airport's 20 gates, with Atlanta-based Delta Airlines operating the other two.

Love Field [had nearly 18 million passengers last year](https://substack.com/redirect/99823779-bc4d-4468-9b4c-cea7e1d5116c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The current terminal complex, designed in 2013, has a designed capacity for 8 million passenger boardings. By 2018, capacity had already exceeded that level, officials said. The increased capacity came on the heels of the 2014 repeal of the Wright Amendment, initially passed in 1979 to restrict the airport so that it could not compete with the larger DFW International Airport. After the law was repealed, Southwest could offer nonstop flights to any destination in the U.S.

Despite the repeal, some [restrictions of the law](https://substack.com/redirect/d984a325-1065-48ae-800a-9b8d7ea0579b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are still enforced through an agreement between the city of Dallas, the city of Fort Worth, Southwest Airlines, American Airlines and the DFW Airport Board.

The agreement stipulates that Love Field is limited to 20 gates, cannot offer international passenger service and had to negotiate a voluntary noise curfew with commercial airlines. According to Carreno, the airport is the only one in the country with a federal law restricting the number of gates it can have.

Despite the restrictions, Love Field is now the [32nd busiest airport in the U.S](https://substack.com/redirect/a16a1c8a-1e9f-4d77-ba10-f06730de5873?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The airport forecasts it could eventually handle 11.9 million passenger boardings and nearly 24 million passengers total under the gate cap, Carreno said.

"We think that's what is possible," Carreno said. "So this program is looking at how we get ourselves to that activity level."

Carreno said Love Field could get close to reaching those passenger estimates by early next decade. "That's why we want to fast-track this program and make sure that we can accommodate and continue to add passengers into this airport."" [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/c9b2b834-a558-49a8-9eda-c86a2a414ea6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)
* * *
**FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED** _: As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. Free subscribers will need to become paid subscribers to receive full story summaries._

* * *
* **2026**

With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed." - Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm (R-TX) AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to , a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin? | | ---|---|--- Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here: . TOP NEWS " Love Field unveils overhaul plan that could add 50% more passengers," Dallas Morning News' Jordan Parker \-- " leaders are calling for a major overhaul to expand the airport in an attempt to squeeze more passengers into the size-restricted air hub. Plans call for expanding the terminal, building a new facility for TSA security checkpoints and new baggage claim and adding a fourth parking garage, among other changes. That could help the airport increase passenger traffic by almost 50% while keeping the legally mandated 20 gates. The expansion program, dubbed , is entering its final stages of development and to the Dallas City Council last week. The airport, which is owned by the city of Dallas and hosts the headquarters for , is now determining the financial feasibility of the project and final layout details. Patrick Carreno , the airport's aviation director, told The Dallas Morning News that the Dallas Love Field Expansion Airport Program's includes a 50-foot extension of the existing terminal, a consolidated rental car facility, a new terminal headhouse for ticketing, TSA security checkpoints and baggage claim, a new parking garage and a commercial pickup area for transportation companies such as Uber and Lyft. A skybridge will connect passengers between security and the main terminal, Carreno said. New access roadways and elevated roadways to improve the flow of traffic around the airport are also part of the plans. Officials that the plan would "enhance capacity, elevate the passenger experience and prepare the City of Dallas-owned airport for continued long-term growth." A team will begin designing the project early next year, with "full construction" set to begin in the first quarter of 2027, Carreno said. "We don't have the full timeline yet. We're looking at full completion towards the end of the decade, early next decade, to finish all 14 components of the LEAP program." Carreno said the project does not have a price tag yet, though he admitted it will be "significantly higher" than the $800 million that the airlines operating at the airport already agreed to pay for their portion of the costs. The previous major improvement project for Love Field, the Love Field Modernization Program, was valued at $519 million, officials said. | | ---|---|--- A screenshot of a video of preliminary plans to redesign Dallas Love Field to accommodate more passengers, which was presented to Dallas officials last week. (Courtesy Dallas Love Field) The announcement of the plan comes three months after . The carrier operates 18 of the airport's 20 gates, with Atlanta-based Delta Airlines operating the other two. Love Field . The current terminal complex, designed in 2013, has a designed capacity for 8 million passenger boardings. By 2018, capacity had already exceeded that level, officials said. The increased capacity came on the heels of the 2014 repeal of the Wright Amendment, initially passed in 1979 to restrict the airport so that it could not compete with the larger DFW International Airport. After the law was repealed, Southwest could offer nonstop flights to any destination in the U.S. Despite the repeal, some are still enforced through an agreement between the city of Dallas, the city of Fort Worth, Southwest Airlines, American Airlines and the DFW Airport Board. The agreement stipulates that Love Field is limited to 20 gates, cannot offer international passenger service and had to negotiate a voluntary noise curfew with commercial airlines. According to Carreno, the airport is the only one in the country with a federal law restricting the number of gates it can have. Despite the restrictions, Love Field is now the . The airport forecasts it could eventually handle 11.9 million passenger boardings and nearly 24 million passengers total under the gate cap, Carreno said. "We think that's what is possible," Carreno said. "So this program is looking at how we get ourselves to that activity level." Carreno said Love Field could get close to reaching those passenger estimates by early next decade. "That's why we want to fast-track this program and make sure that we can accommodate and continue to add passengers into this airport."" Making the best school year with these great back to school tips!

The new school year is here. Are you ready? Preparing to start a fresh school year is so much more than gathering the right
pencils and folder tabs. We want our preparation to reach our children's hearts and prepare them to learn and to serve.

This week, we are sharing back to school tips for a mama's heart. From praying with our children, to incorporating patriotism
every day, here are ten tips for making the best of the year ahead.

Lisa Ekman joins the Moms for America Podcast to discuss her transformation from a leftist Democrat to a conservative patriot. For
more than 20 years, Lisa worked as a Democrat lobbyist and operative in Washington, D.C. She even cried on the night Donald Trump
was elected in 2016.

Hear about the events that opened her eyes to what she now describes as the "progressive cult." Lisa escaped, and now she is
showing the way for other liberals. Lisa is the author of "Deprogramming Democrats and unEducating the Elites." Like, subscribe
and share.

clean_body: Are you ready for the new school year? We're sharing tips to prepare our children's hearts and get the year off to a great start! If you are having trouble viewing this email click here to view in your browser. [] [] [] August 26, 2025 • Newsletter Moms for America Weekly Blog [] [] Making the best school year with these great back to school tips! The new school year is here. Are you ready? Preparing to start a fresh school year is so much more than gathering the right pencils and folder tabs. We want our preparation to reach our children's hearts and prepare them to learn and to serve. This week, we are sharing back to school tips for a mama's heart. From praying with our children, to incorporating patriotism every day, here are ten tips for making the best of the year ahead. Read the Blog [] Liberal Operative to Conservative Patriot Moms for America Weekly Podcast [] [] Moms for America Weekly Podcast [] Lisa Ekman joins the Moms for America Podcast to discuss her transformation from a leftist Democrat to a conservative patriot. For more than 20 years, Lisa worked as a Democrat lobbyist and operative in Washington, D.C. She even cried on the night Donald Trump was elected in 2016. Hear about the events that opened her eyes to what she now describes as the "progressive cult." Lisa escaped, and now she is showing the way for other liberals. Lisa is the author of "Deprogramming Democrats and unEducating the Elites." Like, subscribe and share. View the Podcast [] Listen to the Podcast [] Listen to the Podcast on Spotify [] [] In Truth & Liberty, Kimberly Fletcher Founder and President Moms for America® The Mom View [] [] Empowering Conversations Start Here! From parenting to public policy, The MomView delves into the topics that shape our culture, impact our nation and influence our homes, because every issue is a mom issue. Season 2 Episodes on Mondays at 10am ET View on YouTube [] View on Rumble [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] Copyright © 2025 Moms for America, All rights reserved. Our mailing address is: Moms for America 1440 State HWY 248, Suite Q304 Branson, MO 65616 Visit our Website [] Visit our Store [] Visit us on Truth Social [] DONATE TODAY []

[**MESSAGE FROM NAVY SEALS TIM SHEEHY, ELI CRANE, & RYAN ZINKE**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=7db4f0fc55ac4e13b991bd94b6f16b8c&_e=w5M3KHJhqVgRwH65kC60C_QAHYsxF5KVzd4niSFl3DWbv93rONjbm1rQEJrlajSUf_1q5Tff7IYS5UjLn5Sr5LUH6NCdqVt-SOSZ3sgn5jo6oCTYAY6T_1FnmzSBK6AfVNoREzJmk9Z_5RIknwIKXYBbpKJ5hynEtQP0iBopDs-SEJyOLEGPSh6PjiXU84SiEMX_uw28qMzJ_QcWZNb0oI4PK11R8bIw7ink9_RUJoWO4ZdCT-z4jnM1mjqaF9PJvJtJbw7-gFXjfctURCFVCTbgFT8wFocCrMRSBWZvPv1ENzL1FPAe1voh9Kpyroeubn4lz3Fj5H9WC5hvd0-65aNXHiP2CnYlwbnsrzbk2Hd0C0J9kWV03WIAvmH7D3lwBBVY87EK7qeh_oIQ1TcngEj5Onr16KpRxiSKkcWlgQ6-BqvknjwmDkzJuW5KU8-Evlt1O1vH8P7Ep0hWM0V_lNcmxUPZiBlsjBQGWY6TTzQ-9RooTlAYFT4y25VIG4C72wf20zj_4-o40ySNjCfPToXanf-XS6OufQpvv2lzvsqDKiQXMi0n9k7eTrewUJpTiHM65ahZsLsxL-ZjDLWwvc71kZWLBt0N0skp5HuXh5vw6h02gwo0tn2VuGh3qBcfXpIwNk5bmDJGBbGipvsDbRtPhEcjVe6SlGVBei0FUcX325qDqsr-UIYD8KjRAN3OvJSDfEJRRTfy2rpdIetgWw%3D%3D)
Fellow Conservative,

Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway.

The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs.

We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country.

_**Yet the liberal news corporations, who _desperately_ want to keep the status quo, _attacked us_ for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government.**_

At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway. The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country. Yet the liberal news corporations, who desperately want to keep the status quo, attacked us for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government. All of us –Navy SEALs Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke – are teaming up to stop career politicians and lazy bureaucrats, but we're up against more than just the DC establishment. name: The Democrats
subject: A longer email about how we win back Congress:
body:

Derek, This is a longer email than usual — but with so much on the line right now, we hope you’ll keep reading. **If you don’t have time, we’ll cut to the chase:** [**Democrats have a real shot to take back Congress — but only if we get to work now. name: Tori Colgan
subject: Planned Parenthood did THIS?
body:
For decades, Planned Parenthood has staked its business on taking lives.

| | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/u/hds_LakY67VIdldYCILZ_aZ3xP7ikbTmefy73gARB5KeyPS2wgpc8ZjPIGttjEZn67G0RdCK-THgrmSbW4RvhrkPz5wVkPB3warG-lv94yHdWihvywmMWPXKIWSE21OrcoSRhqdY4m32goLwPwxJFRxeMSi-9UqxQsXJDRmMQt5BkutqWOyBsZnJ/d2bqZ7lY9vUyFoCJoOREFhSwF5uPF3YS/12)_

Dear Fellow American,

For decades, Planned Parenthood has staked its business on taking lives.

Thanks to the billions of dollars it's received in taxpayer funds--including yours--it's the nation's largest abortion business.

**According to a recent _New York Times_ expose, Planned Parenthood's single-minded focus on abortion has led to serious problems at its facilities:**

The article reported filthy clinics, untrained staff, reckless medical care that leaves women injured and scarred--physically and emotionally.

In one story from the article, a woman reported that her failed abortion resulted in weeks of pain, bleeding, and eventually an emergency delivery of a baby who later died.

Many of its facilities have fallen into disrepair. In one facility, sewage seeped into the abortion recovery room for days, making patients physically ill.

**These stories make my stomach turn.**

And, unfortunately, it doesn't stop there. Planned Parenthood's track record on puberty blockers and cross-sex hormones is equally disturbing.

Planned Parenthood has become the nation's _second-largest distributor of "gender-transition" drugs._ It boasts about giving these drugs to minors.

Think about that: Planned Parenthood profits from pumping vulnerable, confused, and hurting kids with drugs that can permanently damage their bodies.

And I haven't even mentioned the organization's devastating legacy of racism and eugenics. Needless to say, Planned Parenthood has been riddled with scandal _since its inception._

Fortunately, I have some exciting news to share:

**The U.S. Supreme Court recently sided with life and allowed states to DEFUND Planned Parenthood and instead support real, comprehensive healthcare providers.**

That means states across the country could protect more women, children, and unborn lives from Planned Parenthood's horrific actions.

Planned Parenthood doesn't deserve another dime of public funds--our hard-earned money.

Please join me in calling on all 50 states to refuse to give Planned Parenthood one more cent from their taxpayers.
[Stop Planned Parenthood](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/_q02b5Z-Xdf4PBs1tcMwP7ZquBh9cuxfgngIQ3gGpqejJPWFVj2U9m2CnnWyXL-KYT2189fri-sqe9GEkJyraPOqiODxmjJbX8eiWvL-i8tXk18hPZUdvigW6riBdkZgAGkrrBjIM99Trbu8Wl6drIjlP9XDhieIDw5EwF2PsaRbZOfdW-hNm4I9Bt9e3_x9y1zFGSFTstvGAWyQd4IpRS7wJyuf8LtJ_P3JRzJD8IvvYjKN1a18Yl2cM6JEjmZWLIpIWBa-ZD2shPj8c7UrDJtGoK2n0oVhnKg5F-7SKr0tw-5rr7f2zJFwSCCmKdJunVTCYgxba3cxMXnA8PvBuV45tfFSK6GnXriTIo4EdheZJaju-MWJgip4BY6pZKHHtaqQQ_gsmrclvLq7SBbVt45j3F23tu3_RwCyKqc02Qh52ih4aYKNfoSqJ_UIsEH93tp0R5_BjuzPYLaolmarNfGMPPGWj8LB47BqmnO1CafR9F2yoYRv1ghKQd811_JXZflcG7TaXasY6t5z9hgcJHt93qUbzTeKbJn0QACs_YZ9PxbDguvQFbxkhNDnYD2LGmjZiyIOsd51YQ92JT005oSxYcvoGkfONnUWkeZzxVEm_n8CNZ_v4aQKbScqPZfxxZRGcFyEZ6OURpQBJJgzUCgIJZkRHGti_gmW0b2W8tSDkbvpyWSMA-42vDmmD_pF-_j8BGEH8l7arKkwdKBxm0n8p08pjYo/LFulScCo5bivJVPLL1zt1lzHm4BCnstz/12)

Let's send a message, loud and clear: Americans DO NOT want our taxpayer dollars to fund Big Abortion. Thank you for your support.

For life,

Tori Colgan
Director of Digital Fundraising

subject: When Child Protection Becomes "Censorship": How Idaho's Unanimous Anti-Porn Bill Exposed a Troubling Disconnect in…
body:
H498 drove Pornhub out of Idaho, yet one prominent org called it a bad bill. What does this say about ideological purity versus practical governance?

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.._DXVXTVbPT241WDmWhXOfPfp4S0HkT3uykufQ5XgZCQ?) for more
---
# [When Child Protection Becomes "Censorship": How Idaho's Unanimous Anti-Porn Bill Exposed a Troubling Disconnect in Conservative Politics](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=171993300&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTkzMzAwLCJpYXQiOjE3NTYyMjM1NDAsImV4cCI6MTc1ODgxNTU0MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vHsSmq07j4Olp1u4qwUec-Bg2e0em3OfBgxeZWRX3X4)
### H498 drove Pornhub out of Idaho, yet one prominent org called it a bad bill. What does this say about ideological purity versus practical governance?
| | [Brian Lenney - Nampa's Senator](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---
| Aug 26
---
| [](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=171993300&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTkzMzAwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NjIyMzU0MCwiZXhwIjoxNzU4ODE1NTQwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjExNDgyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.UgJFUtxNISGBT8CB9H1gVNgUf0luHHaswhMjCQUm1rI&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=171993300&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTkzMzAwLCJpYXQiOjE3NTYyMjM1NDAsImV4cCI6MTc1ODgxNTU0MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vHsSmq07j4Olp1u4qwUec-Bg2e0em3OfBgxeZWRX3X4&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=171993300&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTkzMzAwLCJpYXQiOjE3NTYyMjM1NDAsImV4cCI6MTc1ODgxNTU0MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vHsSmq07j4Olp1u4qwUec-Bg2e0em3OfBgxeZWRX3X4)
---
| | [](https://substack..MWrvt33mT0lVpg4ggbUkpp02bYtzp6-AQEDm_8oAVdA?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/brianlenney/p/when-child-protection-becomes-censorship?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Take a look at this:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ec5bf46f-3fb3-406a-9a13-c39c9a856292?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
That post is from the chief bill rater for the Idaho Freedom Foundation. So, you guessed it, the Idaho Freedom Foundation rated [H498 from 2024 ](https://substack.com/redirect/d1a65a55-1061-42af-916d-4fb3e1f38fe0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[NEGATIVELY](https://substack.com/redirect/d1a65a55-1061-42af-916d-4fb3e1f38fe0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** :
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7d0ad19a-d647-442d-95a0-ebd9b48f86b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Hard to believe, right?

This is one of many reasons why I'm a _conservtaive_ , not a Libertarian. Because this was a bill that passed with every single legislator voting yes, even the Democrats. I think it was the most consequential bill of the year, personally.
Why? Because this bill got sites like Pornhub to flee the state. Yes, you cannot look at Pornhub in Idaho anymore. It required the porn sites (not the device makers) to verify age before showing obscene content. So organizations like Pornhub said, "BYE BYE Idaho."
This is what their website shows if you try to view it:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b3df8b72-cafd-48bb-ad31-aa192b073091?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This was a HUGE WIN for Idaho...
Yet, the Idaho Freedom Foundation thought this was a _**bad bill**._
So herein lies the problem, right? The chief bill rater for the IFF says that the bill we passed last year, H498, which got _**100% legislative support**_ to keep porn away from kids was actually some sinister censorship plot?
He really thinks protecting children from explicit content is about "silencing truth?"
What exactly is the "raging river of truth" he's defending here? Kids accessing hardcore pornography? Because that's what he's calling "censorship."
Furthermore, his own organization's metrics say they support bills that protect "the innocence of children" and prevent "promoting degeneracy."
Well, H498 literally does both of those things with unanimous legislative support.
So either their rating system missed the mark here, or there's a disconnect between stated principles and actual policy evaluation.
_**Look, this is just one example of why folks shouldn't treat any single political organization as the final word on what's right for Idaho.**_
The IFF does valuable work on many issues, but they're not infallible.
When they rate a unanimous child protection bill negatively while claiming to defend traditional values and children's innocence, that should make people think twice about blind deference to any scorecard.
Sometimes a spade is just a spade, and keeping porn away from minors isn't some grand conspiracy. It's basic child protection that somehow got lost in absolutist libertarian ideology.

---

clean_body: H498 drove Pornhub out of Idaho, yet one prominent org called it a bad bill. What does this say about ideological purity versus practical governance? | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more H498 drove Pornhub out of Idaho, yet one prominent org called it a bad bill. What does this say about ideological purity versus practical governance? | | | Aug 26 | | | | | | | | | | | | Take a look at this: | | ---|---|--- That post is from the chief bill rater for the Idaho Freedom Foundation. So, you guessed it, the Idaho Freedom Foundation rated : | | ---|---|--- Hard to believe, right? This is one of many reasons why I'm a conservtaive , not a Libertarian. Because this was a bill that passed with every single legislator voting yes, even the Democrats. I think it was the most consequential bill of the year, personally. Why? Because this bill got sites like Pornhub to flee the state. Yes, you cannot look at Pornhub in Idaho anymore. It required the porn sites (not the device makers) to verify age before showing obscene content. So organizations like Pornhub said, "BYE BYE Idaho." This is what their website shows if you try to view it: | | ---|---|--- This was a HUGE WIN for Idaho... Yet, the Idaho Freedom Foundation thought this was a bad bill. So herein lies the problem, right? The chief bill rater for the IFF says that the bill we passed last year, H498, which got 100% legislative support to keep porn away from kids was actually some sinister censorship plot? He really thinks protecting children from explicit content is about "silencing truth?" What exactly is the "raging river of truth" he's defending here? Kids accessing hardcore pornography? Because that's what he's calling "censorship." Furthermore, his own organization's metrics say they support bills that protect "the innocence of children" and prevent "promoting degeneracy." Well, H498 literally does both of those things with unanimous legislative support. So either their rating system missed the mark here, or there's a disconnect between stated principles and actual policy evaluation. Look, this is just one example of why folks shouldn't treat any single political organization as the final word on what's right for Idaho. The IFF does valuable work on many issues, but they're not infallible. When they rate a unanimous child protection bill negatively while claiming to defend traditional values and children's innocence, that should make people think twice about blind deference to any scorecard. Sometimes a spade is just a spade, and keeping porn away from minors isn't some grand conspiracy. It's basic child protection that somehow got lost in absolutist libertarian ideology. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Brian Lenney ? ?

| ''SEPTA is broken. “Team SEPTA” wants us to believe the issue is that they’re _broke_ (and deserve even more taxpayer aid), but the sad reality is the system is _broken_. Nothing that SEPTA officials, its Board of Directors comprised of politicians, its politician allies, mass-transit cultists, anti-car activists, climate warriors, or their apologists in the legacy media — and, even those honest to goodness riders genuinely concerned about getting to work — can say will change that unfortunate reality..."

Guy draws on decades of experience from South Philly to the Philadelphia suburbs to Pittsburgh, from Harrisburg to Washington, DC, having served at the highest levels of government, as a candidate and strategist, as well as Chamber CEO. Guy has been listening, observing and guiding his readers for years.

name: Dan Goldman
subject: Trump rolled out the red carpet for Putin
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York**
body:
|
#
---
Peter, Earlier this month, Donald Trump met with Vladimir Putin in Alaska and **literally** rolled out the red carpet for him. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114033493/565725676/1699199121?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250826_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTA4OTc2Ni05NjgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=epEbcxeVuTwrB-XC0POgEphwV6Z5q-2_1KRlTCyZ5Wo=) _Photo: Doug Mills/The New York Times_ Trump failed to secure an agreement after claiming that Putin wanted to “make a deal for” him. Instead, Trump treated Putin like a visiting king, not the dictator of a foreign adversary who has committed war crimes. This isn’t new. Trump has a long history of putting Vladimir Putin’s interests above America’s. Putin helped Trump win the 2016 election — and since then, Trump has been nothing more than Putin’s puppet by:
* Trying to build Trump Tower Moscow while serving as President
* Choosing to believe Putin over our country’s intelligence community in 2018
* Withholding critical aid from Ukraine in 2019
* Shipping COVID testing kits to Putin at the height of the pandemic

The reason is simple: Trump idolizes Putin — a dictator with absolute power, unaccountable to anyone, especially the people of his country. As Democrats’ lead counsel during Trump’s first impeachment, I saw firsthand how Donald Trump only cares about himself, not the public interest. In his second term, he has used the presidency to line his own pockets, rip away health care from millions, and bend the knee to foreign dictators while destabilizing democracy at home and abroad. **[We can’t sit back while Trump undermines our democracy for personal gain. I’m working hard to hold him and his allies accountable in the House. Can you chip in today to help me keep standing up to this administration?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114033495/565725677/1699199121?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250826_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTA4OTc2Ni05NjgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=epEbcxeVuTwrB-XC0POgEphwV6Z5q-2_1KRlTCyZ5Wo=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114033496/565725678/1699199121?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250826_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTA4OTc2Ni05NjgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=epEbcxeVuTwrB-XC0POgEphwV6Z5q-2_1KRlTCyZ5Wo=)

clean_body: | Peter, Earlier this month, Donald Trump met with Vladimir Putin in Alaska and literally rolled out the red carpet for him. = Photo: Doug Mills/The New York Times Trump failed to secure an agreement after claiming that Putin wanted to “make a deal for” him. Instead, Trump treated Putin like a visiting king, not the dictator of a foreign adversary who has committed war crimes. This isn’t new. Trump has a long history of putting Vladimir Putin’s interests above America’s. Putin helped Trump win the 2016 election — and since then, Trump has been nothing more than Putin’s puppet by: Trying to build Trump Tower Moscow while serving as President Choosing to believe Putin over our country’s intelligence community in 2018 Withholding critical aid from Ukraine in 2019 Shipping COVID testing kits to Putin at the height of the pandemic The reason is simple: Trump idolizes Putin — a dictator with absolute power, unaccountable to anyone, especially the people of his country. As Democrats’ lead counsel during Trump’s first impeachment, I saw firsthand how Donald Trump only cares about himself, not the public interest. In his second term, he has used the presidency to line his own pockets, rip away health care from millions, and bend the knee to foreign dictators while destabilizing democracy at home and abroad. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Together, we must defend our democracy and ensure those in power answer to the American people, not a foreign tyrant. — Dan Dan Goldman for New York PO BOX 3306 New York, NY 10008 United States | mailto:info@dangoldmanforny.com | Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York

name: Mark Kelly
subject: My take on the Senate special elections.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | Hi, DSCC Vice Chair Mark Kelly here. I want to talk to you about a _key_ opportunity in front of us as we fight for the Senate majority: **special elections.**

I know a thing or two about these kinds of races, because I got to the Senate by _winning_ a special election back in 2020 — even as Republicans spent over $70 million to stop me. **And my victory proved mission-critical, because without it, Democrats wouldn't have had a Senate majority.**

[**Now, history could repeat itself. Two special elections coming up in Ohio and Florida are among a small handful of races that could decide the majority — again. So I'm coming to ask you, will you donate $5 to win races like those and take back the Senate? >>**](https://go.dscc.org/go/21844?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250826-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250826-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&akid=21076%2E9716520%2ErXa5GS)

| | [Donate $5](https://go.dscc.org/go/21844?amount=5&t=8&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250826-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250826-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&akid=21076%2E9716520%2ErXa5GS)
---

I want to be clear, team: **These special elections give us an opportunity we would have waited YEARS for without them, and they make a BIG difference.**

In fact, during Donald Trump's first term, Democrats flipped **_three_** Senate seats in special elections, including mine, my good friend Raphael Warnock's, and even a seat in dark-red Alabama. Each time, we defeated Trump-endorsed candidates who would have been guaranteed votes for his destructive agenda, and instead we elected Democrats who fought for hardworking Americans.

**Now, we're facing the same task again — but it won't be easy.** Trump is reportedly "obsessed" with the midterms, National Republicans will spend big-time to keep their majority, and just like when I first won, Democrats only need to flip a handful of seats to take back the Senate.

**Take it from someone who's been in this position before: We'll need your help to make the most of this opportunity — and that's why I'm reaching out.**

**[As a DSCC Vice Chair, I'm personally asking: Will you chip in $5 or whatever you can to help Democrats win in states like Ohio and Florida and take back the Senate? We have a real opportunity to topple Trump's majority, and we can't let it slip away >>](https://go.dscc.org/go/21844?amount=5&t=9&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250826-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250826-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&akid=21076%2E9716520%2ErXa5GS)**

We need your help to fight for the Senate. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

| [Donate $5](https://go.dscc.org/go/21844?amount=5&t=10&amount=5&express_lane=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250826-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250826-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&akid=21076%2E9716520%2ErXa5GS)

clean_body: | | | Hi, DSCC Vice Chair Mark Kelly here. I want to talk to you about a key opportunity in front of us as we fight for the Senate majority: special elections. I know a thing or two about these kinds of races, because I got to the Senate by winning a special election back in 2020 — even as Republicans spent over $70 million to stop me. And my victory proved mission-critical, because without it, Democrats wouldn't have had a Senate majority. | | I want to be clear, team: These special elections give us an opportunity we would have waited YEARS for without them, and they make a BIG difference. In fact, during Donald Trump's first term, Democrats flipped three Senate seats in special elections, including mine, my good friend Raphael Warnock's, and even a seat in dark-red Alabama. Each time, we defeated Trump-endorsed candidates who would have been guaranteed votes for his destructive agenda, and instead we elected Democrats who fought for hardworking Americans. Now, we're facing the same task again — but it won't be easy. Trump is reportedly "obsessed" with the midterms, National Republicans will spend big-time to keep their majority, and just like when I first won, Democrats only need to flip a handful of seats to take back the Senate. Take it from someone who's been in this position before: We'll need your help to make the most of this opportunity — and that's why I'm reaching out. We need your help to fight for the Senate. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately. | | | | Thank you for your support. name: Bob Brooks
subject: I’m running to defeat Ryan Mackenzie
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRIS DELUZIO FOR CONGRESS
body:
I’m Bob Brooks. I’m a firefighter, a dad, and someone who has spent his life working hard for myself, my family, and my community.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114034967/565730851/-1883187425?refcode=email_082625_Brooks-split&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNTFiZjJlOS05NzgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=I5Bw-PPeZsJdwHfQlIPgf-OYVFcvZHc2PyH3GnIyfkc=) Derek, I’m Bob Brooks. I’m a firefighter, a dad, and someone who has spent his life working hard for myself, my family, and my community. **Today, I’m announcing my campaign to defeat Republican Ryan Mackenzie in PA-07. Last year, he won this race by just one percentage point. Next year, we have the chance to defeat him.** [**I want to tell you a bit more about who I am and why I’m running for Congress, but if you’re already with me in the fight to flip this competitive seat, then I’m humbly asking you to split a donation between Chris Deluzio and me →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114034968/565730858/-1883187425?refcode=email_082625_Brooks-split&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNTFiZjJlOS05NzgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=I5Bw-PPeZsJdwHfQlIPgf-OYVFcvZHc2PyH3GnIyfkc=) | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114034970/565730866/-1883187425?refcode=email_082625_Brooks-split&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNTFiZjJlOS05NzgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=I5Bw-PPeZsJdwHfQlIPgf-OYVFcvZHc2PyH3GnIyfkc=)
---
I faced a lot of obstacles at home as a kid. My childhood was a mix of challenges and hard work. My mom was a bartender and my dad was a mason. But in the early years, he served time in prison, so my mom raised me mostly on her own until she remarried.
I was a kid who loved sports — football, baseball (I played every position except pitcher and catcher), basketball, wrestling.
I went to college mostly because I wanted to play baseball. But an injury ended my career, and I dropped out soon after.
That’s why, from a young age, I worked plenty of different jobs. Paperboy, fry cook, dishwasher, bartender, pizza delivery driver — you name it, I did it. I know what it’s like to clock in early, work hard, and still wonder if you’re getting ahead.
**But in 2005, I found my passion: I became a firefighter with the city of Bethlehem.**
I threw myself into that job. I did every job the Fire Department had — I started out driving Truck 2, drove under three different chiefs, moved on to drive Ladder 2, and sometimes served as a hoseman. From the start, I was deeply involved in the firefighters’ union, serving as secretary, vice president, and president.
I spent 20 years there because fighting fires was more than just a job for me — it was about serving my community and standing up for the people who keep us safe.
I’ve worked hard. I’ve struggled. And I’ve lived through the hardships that too many of my fellow Pennsylvanians know — but that politicians never will.
**That’s exactly why I’m running for Congress.**
Republicans in Congress like Ryan Mackenzie are spending their time prioritizing Donald Trump and the wealthy billionaires who back their campaigns.
While families in PA-07 struggle with rising costs and making ends meet, Mackenzie is busy supporting policies that slash Medicaid and SNAP to pay for tax cuts to the rich and powerful.
We deserve better. We deserve someone who understands what working class families and small business owners are going through. We need someone who knows what it means to work hard and still wonder if you’re going to get by.
I’m that person, Derek. I’m running so that I can fight for you, the people.
**[So if you believe we need real representation in Congress that puts the people of PA-07 first, I’m asking for your support. Please, make a split donation between Chris Deluzio and me today →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114034971/565730873/-1883187425?refcode=email_082625_Brooks-split&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNTFiZjJlOS05NzgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=I5Bw-PPeZsJdwHfQlIPgf-OYVFcvZHc2PyH3GnIyfkc=)**

## Leftist language backfires...
Ever heard of the left-wing group "Third Way"?
It hasn't been in the news much, until recently.
But they caught my attention when they sent out a memo that said the quiet part out loud:
The Left's language is losing them voters.
Let's be honest.
The Democrats and mainstream media have been weaponizing language for decades.
It's how they kept their narrative front and center--and how they silenced or cancelled people who dared to push back.
But with the rise of alternative media and platforms for free discourse like X, that strategy is backfiring big time.
Americans are seeing through the charade.
They're not just seeing through it... the language is now turning them away from the Democrat Party.
And now the Left is panicking.
Let's break it all down. ...

Dear Patriot,

Will you rush back your ****

IMPEACH FEDERAL JUDGE FARBIARZ urgent reply in the next 24 hours?

My name is Larry Klayman.

You may know me from my days suing Hillary Clinton, Obama, George Soros, and even the leaders of the “Black Lives Matter” movement.

I’ve dedicated my life to **tearing down walls of corruption** so Americans like you can see the deep state cockroaches scurry out into the light.

Today, my mission is simple:

He is the **RAT IN THE ROBE** who ordered the release of Mahmoud Khalil – the Pro-Hamas “student” who lied on his green card application and **INCITED RIOTS** at Columbia University.

Why would he lie?

Because Mahmoud Khalil is not just some “protester” — he’s a militant sympathizer who proudly led the mob down the streets of New York as they chanted

**DEATH TO AMERICA!**

He stood against our country, against our flag, and against everything we hold sacred.

And now Judge Farbiarz, sworn to uphold the Constitution, is protecting him — **defying President Trump’s orders** and unleashing this agent of terror back onto our streets.

This isn’t just betrayal — it’s an existential threat to our homeland.

Not only must Khalil be locked up and deported...

But federal judges like Michael Farbiarz must be **IMPEACHED** and permanently removed from the bench.

**That’s where you come in.** I can’t do this alone. My team and I are launching a full-blown national pressure campaign — and that takes immediate grassroots action.

Will you rush back your **IMPEACH JUDGE FARBIARZ **urgent reply with $25 or more in the next 24 hours?

Thank you for your time and support.

For Our Sacred Republic,

******Larry Klayman** Founder, Freedom Watch ****
